name: James Joyce
abstract: James Augustine Aloysius Joyce (born James Augusta Joyce; 2 February 1882 – 13 January 1941) was an Irish novelist, poet, and literary critic. He contributed to the modernist movement and is regarded among the most influential and important writers of the 20th century.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I went and read James Joyce over again, because I had read him when I was in English, because that was my major you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: ?J [inaudible. evidently a reference to James Joyce.] wrote a lot of crazy things, and I’m not sure what value that has.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
